login.title: Front Desk
login.totpHint: Enter the code from your authenticator app
login.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts, wait a moment and try again
login.ssoFailed: Single sign-on failed. Try again or use another login method.

members.title: Members
members.empty: No members yet. Add your first Model Hotel instance below.
members.lastSyncPrimaryTip: This member is the source of config.
members.verifiedTip: Auto-sync confirmed this member matches the primary {{when}}. This ticks while auto-sync is running, even when no config changed.
members.verifiedNeverTip: Auto-sync has not confirmed this member against the primary yet, or the member is currently unreachable.
members.verifiedPrimaryTip: This member is the primary; other members are verified in sync against it.
members.primaryTip: The fleet's config source. Sync runs push this member's providers, virtual keys, and synced settings to every other member.
members.drainTip: Stop sending new traffic to this instance. In-flight requests finish, then it leaves the load-balancer pool. Use before maintenance or removal.
members.activateTip: Return this instance to the load-balancer pool so it receives traffic again.
members.removeTip: Stop tracking this instance and drop it from the pool. The instance itself is left running and untouched.
members.syncHeldTip: This member runs a different app version than the primary, so config sync is held to avoid overwriting its settings.
members.tokenHint: Required. Stored encrypted. Front Desk verifies the host with it on add, and uses it for version and traffic readings and admin-token sync.
members.removeBody: Front Desk stops tracking {{name}} and removes it from the Traefik backend pool. The instance itself is not touched. This does not drain in-flight requests; drain first for zero-loss removal.
members.firstMemberPrimary: This is your first member, so it is the default sync primary: the instance whose providers, virtual keys, and settings the rest of the fleet will copy. Supply each member's own dashboard admin token when you add it; to sign in to every dashboard with one password, set the same ADMIN_TOKEN on each member. This is the dashboard admin token, not your Front Desk login or the MASTER_KEY.
members.errHttpsRequired: URLs must use https. Set FRONTDESK_ALLOW_HTTP_MEMBERS=true on the Front Desk container to allow plain http on a trusted internal network.
members.errDuplicate: A member with this URL already exists.
members.confirmTokenPlaceholder: Enter the Front Desk admin token
members.errAlreadyPrimary: This host is already the fleet primary and cannot be added as a member. Change the primary from the Fleet Sync wizard instead.
members.errUnreachable: Front Desk could not reach this host to verify it. Check the URL and admin token, and that the host is running.
members.errIdentityUnverified: Front Desk verified the admin token but could not read this host's fleet identity to confirm it is not the primary or an existing member. Check the host and try again.
members.errAlreadyMember: This host is already a member (added under a different address). Remove the existing entry first if you want to re-add it.

traffic.title: Traffic
traffic.intro: Per-member request and error rates, pulled from each member's own metrics. Use it to confirm load is actually being distributed.
traffic.noTokenMembers: Traffic needs a stored admin token for each member. Add a token on the Members tab to chart that member here.
traffic.empty: No members with a stored admin token yet.
traffic.unreachable: Could not read metrics from this member

settings.title: Settings

observability.title: Observability
observability.description: Log-export integrations, each enabled by an environment variable and reflected here read-only.

json.name: JSON logs
json.description: Emit one JSON object per log line for external collectors.
json.instructions: Set this environment variable and restart Front Desk to switch stdout logs to JSON.

otel.name: OTLP log export
otel.description: Ship structured log records to an OpenTelemetry collector.
otel.instructions: Point Front Desk at your collector with the standard OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_* variables and restart.

metrics.name: Prometheus metrics
metrics.description: Serve fleet, poller, and sync metrics for Prometheus at /metrics.
metrics.instructions: Set a dedicated scrape token and restart Front Desk, then point your Prometheus scrape config at /metrics with that bearer token. Without a token the endpoint still works behind the admin login.

oidc.title: Single sign-on (OIDC)
oidc.hint: Let admins sign in through an external OpenID Connect provider, a fourth login path alongside the Front Desk token, TOTP, and passkeys. Any standards-compliant provider works, as long as it releases the user's verified email. GitHub login is configured on the main dashboard only.
oidc.issuerHint: The provider's base URL. Its discovery document is read from <issuer>/.well-known/openid-configuration.
oidc.clientSecretHint: Stored encrypted. Paste the secret from the OAuth client you register with your provider.
oidc.secretStoredNote: A secret is stored. Leave unchanged to keep it, type a new one to replace it, or clear the field to remove it.
oidc.baseUrlHint: Front Desk's own externally reachable base URL. The redirect URI below is built from it.
oidc.redirectUriHint: Register this exact URI with your provider as the OAuth client's redirect URI.
oidc.allowedEmailsHint: Comma-separated allowlist of verified email addresses permitted to sign in. Fail-closed: an empty list denies everyone.
oidc.fallbackNote: SSO is additive: the Front Desk token, TOTP, and passkey logins always keep working, so a misconfigured or unreachable provider cannot lock you out.

settings.pollSectionHint: These regenerate the Traefik config that the data plane polls.
settings.traefikStaleHint: Warn when Traefik has not fetched the config within this window.
settings.retryHint: Retries that failed before any response byte (safe to retry).
settings.healthFailThresholdHint: Consecutive failed health checks before a member is reported down and alerted. Higher tolerates longer rebuilds without flapping. Recovery is immediate.
settings.sessionTimeoutHint: Sign out of Front Desk automatically after this many minutes with no activity. 0 keeps the session open.
settings.configSyncConfirmBody: This makes each member below match the primary's providers, virtual keys, and settings. Items the primary does not have are removed from the member; its request logs and metering are untouched.
settings.configSyncRemovalWarning_one: {{count}} existing item will be removed from members that have it but the primary does not.
settings.configSyncRemovalWarning_other: {{count}} existing items will be removed from members that have them but the primary does not.
settings.configSyncAck: I understand these members' config will be overwritten to match the primary
settings.configSyncProgress: Replacing config and rediscovering models on each member. This can take up to a minute; keep this window open.

alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.hint: Send a push notification when something happens in your fleet (a member goes down, a config sync fails). Front Desk posts to an Apprise (apprise-api) container you run; from there it fans out to Telegram, Discord, email, and dozens more.
alerts.apiUrlHint: Base URL of your apprise-api container, reachable from Front Desk (e.g. http://apprise:8000 on the HA network).
alerts.targetHint: One or more Apprise URLs, separated by ; (e.g. tgram://token/chat_id). Stored encrypted.
alerts.targetStoredNote: A target is stored (hidden). Leave as-is to keep it, type a new value to replace it, or clear it to remove.
alerts.eventsHint: Choose which fleet events send a notification.
alerts.ntfyHint: Get fleet alerts on your phone: point a target at an ntfy topic (self-hosted or ntfy.sh with a secret topic) and subscribe to the same topic in the ntfy or Bellhop app.

wizard.title: Fleet sync wizard
wizard.intro: Converge your whole fleet onto one primary, one step at a time. Each step unlocks only once the one before it is done for every reachable instance.
wizard.step1Intro: Pick the instance whose setup the rest of the fleet should copy. Only instances that are online right now can be the primary.
wizard.lastRunBanner: You last synced this fleet {{when}} (primary: {{name}}). Pick a primary below to run it again.
wizard.primaryUnusable: This instance cannot be the primary: {{note}}
wizard.step2Intro: Every instance must share the same MASTER_KEY so encrypted provider keys can be read everywhere. MASTER_KEY is set when each instance is deployed, so it can only be checked here, not changed.
wizard.step2Ok: Every reachable instance shares the primary's MASTER_KEY.
wizard.step2Remedy: Set the same MASTER_KEY environment variable on the instances below and restart them, then re-check:
wizard.schemaRemedy: These instances run an older app version and must be upgraded to match the primary before they can sync. Update and restart them, then re-check:
wizard.step3Intro: Copy the primary's providers, virtual keys, and settings onto the rest of the fleet. Items the primary does not have are removed from the others.
wizard.step3NoChanges: Every reachable instance already matches the primary's configuration.
wizard.versionSkewBlock_one: Sync is on hold: {{count}} member runs a different app version than the primary. Align the versions, then refresh.
wizard.versionSkewBlock_other: Sync is on hold: {{count}} members run a different app version than the primary. Align the versions, then refresh.
wizard.devAckBody: Both sides report the 'dev' version. Dev builds are matched by version string only, not by commit, so keeping them actually aligned is up to you. Proceed?
wizard.configLegendTitle: What the numbers mean (configuration entries: providers, virtual keys, settings):
wizard.configLegendAdded: to add on this instance (only the primary has them)
wizard.configLegendUpdated: to overwrite so they match the primary
wizard.configLegendRemoved: to delete from this instance (the primary does not have them)
wizard.configTipAdded: Add {{count}}: only the primary has these
wizard.configTipRemoved: Remove {{count}}: the primary does not have these
wizard.skippedOffline: These instances are offline and not yet in sync. They converge automatically once they are back:
wizard.doneUseTitle: How to send traffic to your fleet
wizard.doneUseIntro: Front Desk is the control plane; it does not carry /v1 traffic itself. The load balancer spreads requests across every active instance, with health checks and automatic failover. Point your apps at the load balancer, never at a single instance.
wizard.doneDirectTitle: Option A: point clients straight at the load balancer (trusted network)
wizard.doneProxyTitle: Option B: behind a reverse proxy (recommended for anything internet-facing)
wizard.doneProxyForward: Point your reverse proxy (e.g. Nginx Proxy Manager) at this address (no /v1):
wizard.doneProxyClients: Then your apps keep using https://your-public-host/v1. TLS terminates at your proxy; the load balancer itself speaks plain http.
wizard.donePortNote: Port {{port}} comes from LB_PORT in your HA .env. This host was detected from your current Front Desk URL; if the HA stack runs elsewhere, substitute that IP or hostname.
wizard.donePoolTitle: Active instances behind the load balancer:
wizard.donePoolEmpty: No active instances yet. Activate at least one on the Members tab.
wizard.doneHttpsNote: The /v1 data plane works over plain http, streaming included. https matters only for two things: Front Desk's own login (passkeys and 2FA require it) and keeping your keys encrypted in transit. Put TLS in front (Option B) for anything beyond a trusted network.
wizard.restingNeverRun: This primary has not synced the fleet yet.
wizard.autoSyncOnHint: The fleet tracks this primary and re-syncs within seconds of any change on it.
wizard.autoSyncOffHint: Nothing propagates while paused, so the fleet can drift from this primary until you resume.
wizard.rerunWarning: Re-running re-designates the source of truth and replaces every other member's config with the new primary's. Providers, keys, and settings the others have but the new primary does not are removed. Each member is backed up first.
wizard.changeBody: {{name}} will become the primary and auto-sync will track it. Enter the Front Desk admin token to confirm.
wizard.changeTokenNote: Changing the primary replaces the fleet's source of truth, so it needs the admin token.
wizard.confirmTokenPlaceholder: Enter the Front Desk admin token
wizard.sameHostError: You cannot replace the primary with the same host: it is already the fleet primary.

passkeys.intro: Sign in to Front Desk with a hardware security key or a platform authenticator (Touch ID, Windows Hello). Passkeys are stored on this Front Desk instance only and are not synced to members.
passkeys.notConfigured: Passkeys are not configured. Set PUBLIC_ORIGIN to an https:// URL (and reach Front Desk over HTTPS) to enable them.
passkeys.deleteConfirmBody: {{name}} will no longer be able to sign in to Front Desk. This cannot be undone.

totp.intro: Require a 6-digit code from an authenticator app in addition to your Front Desk token. The secret is stored on this Front Desk instance only and is not synced to members.
totp.enableDescription: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app, then enter the 6-digit code to confirm.
totp.disableDescription: Enter a current 6-digit code or a recovery code to turn off two-factor authentication.
totp.failedToDisable: Could not disable, check the code
totp.recoveryWarning: Save these recovery codes somewhere safe. Each works once if you lose your authenticator. They are shown only now.

devices.title: Paired devices
devices.description: Pair the Bellhop phone app (or any device) with a one-time code. Scan the QR or paste the pairing string; the code works once and expires in a few minutes. Each device gets its own revocable token.
devices.roleOperatorHint: Operator devices can also drain or activate members, trigger a config sync, and toggle auto-sync.
devices.roleMonitorHint: Monitor devices are read-only: members, health, traffic, events, and alerts.
devices.expired: This pairing code expired. Generate a new one.
devices.revokeBody: “{{label}}” will immediately lose access. Pair it again to restore it.

errors.unauthorized: Your session expired, please sign in again
